questions.question: Define software quality and explain its two fundamental dimensions.
keyPoints: Software quality is the degree to which software meets specified requirements and user expectations
First dimension: Conformance to specifications (does what it should)
Second dimension: Fitness for use (meets actual user needs in practice)

questions.question: Explain the Cost of Quality (CoQ) model with its four components and their examples in software projects.
keyPoints: Prevention costs: Training, process definition, quality planning
Appraisal costs: Testing, inspection, reviews
Internal failure costs: Rework, scrap before delivery
External failure costs: Support, patches, customer complaints after release

questions.question: Differentiate between Quality Assurance and Quality Control in software engineering.
keyPoints: QA is process-oriented; QC is product-oriented
QA prevents defects; QC detects and fixes defects
QA is proactive; QC is reactive
QA involves all team members; QC is done by specialized testers

questions.question: Describe the 'Iceberg Effect' or 'Defect Amplification' phenomenon in software quality.
keyPoints: One defect in requirements can generate multiple defects in design and code
Early phase defects are cheaper to fix than late-phase defects
Requirement defect found in design costs 10x more than in requirements phase
Design defect found in coding costs 100x more than if caught in design phase

questions.question: List and briefly explain five key software quality attributes (characteristics).
keyPoints: Correctness: Meets specified requirements
Reliability: Performs consistently without failures
Maintainability: Easy to modify and enhance
Efficiency: Uses computational resources properly
Usability: Easy to learn and operate for users

questions.question: Discuss the importance of software quality in project management. Explain how quality planning, assurance, and control contribute to project success with examples.
keyPoints: Quality directly impacts customer satisfaction and project reputation
Higher quality reduces long-term maintenance costs (50-80% of lifecycle costs)
Quality planning: Defines quality objectives and standards early
Quality Assurance: Systematic processes prevent defects through reviews, standards
Quality Control: Testing and inspection detect defects for correction
Poor quality leads to rework, schedule delays, cost overruns, and business loss
ROI of quality: Prevention costs are much lower than failure costs

questions.question: Explain the ISO/IEC 9126 software quality model. Discuss the six main characteristics and their sub-characteristics.
keyPoints: International standard for software quality evaluation
Six main characteristics:
1. Functionality: Suitability, accuracy, interoperability, security, compliance
2. Reliability: Maturity, fault tolerance, recoverability
3. Usability: Understandability, learnability, operability
4. Efficiency: Time behavior, resource utilization
5. Maintainability: Analysability, changeability, stability, testability
6. Portability: Adaptability, installability, conformance, replaceability
Model helps in defining quality requirements and measuring quality
